intro: Schoenocaulon caricifolium, also known as "Tulipe de la Sierra" or "Mexican Tulip Poppy," is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been reported to help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Schoenocaulon caricifolium include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as tulipalin and berberine, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
